About Frontier Developments plc
Frontier is a leading independent developer and publisher of video games founded in 1994 by David Braben, co-author of the iconic Elite game. Based in Cambridge, Frontier uses its proprietary COBRA game development technology to create innovative genre-leading games, primarily for personal computers and video game consoles.

The Company intends to place the purchased shares into Treasury.
Following settlement of the above purchases, Frontier has purchased a total of 26,537 Ordinary Shares since the commencement of the buyback programme and has 39,478,535 Ordinary Shares in issue. latest.title: Launch of on-market Share Buyback Programme
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4341U
Frontier Developments PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
Frontier Developments plc
Launch of on-market Share Buyback Programme
Frontier Developments plc (AIM: FDEV, 'Frontier', the 'Company' or the 'Group'), a leading developer and publisher of video games based in Cambridge, UK, announces today its intention to commence an on-market share buyback programme, (the 'Buyback'), t
o purchase up to 1,429,327 ordinary shares of 0.5 pence each in the Company ("Ordinary Shares")
. The Buyback will begin with immediate effect.
The Buyback, which follows from the completion of an on-market buyback of £10 million in October 2025, reflects the Company's substantial cash generation in the financial year and current financial position, as well as the Board's confidence in Frontier's creative management simulation (CMS) game focussed strategy and future prospects.
The Company considers share buyback programmes to be a part of its capital allocation strategy and an important component of shareholder returns, enhancing return on equity and increasing earnings per share through the return of surplus capital. Future buyback programmes will remain subject to Frontier's ongoing financial performance and capital requirements.
Details of the Buyback
Frontier has appointed Peel Hunt LLP ('Peel Hunt') to execute the Buyback. Under the terms of the engagement, Peel Hunt will act as riskless principal and will independently determine the timing and pricing of purchases within the parameters of the Buyback and without influence from the Company.
The Company has authorised the Buyback to continue until the earlier of: (i) 1,429,327 Ordinary Shares are repurchased, being the balance remaining from the
shareholder approved waiver under Rule 9 of the City Code on Takeovers and Mergers at the Company's General Meeting held on 27 June 2025 ("Rule 9 Waiver"),
(ii) the aggregate consideration payable by the Company has reached £8 million (this threshold having been set by the Board of Directors of the Company), or (iii) 27 June 2026, being the expiry of the Rule 9 Waiver and the share purchase authority.
Any purchase of Ordinary Shares under the Buyback will take place in open market transactions and may be undertaken from time to time depending on market conditions, share price and trading volumes.
The Buyback will be carried out under the general authority granted by shareholders at the 2025 AGM and conducted in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) No 2016/1052, in each case as incorporated into UK domestic law pursuant to the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018. However,
on any given trading day the Buyback may exceed 25 per cent of the average daily trading volume of the Shares. Accordingly, the Company may not benefit from the exemption contained in Article 5(1) of Regulation (EU) 596/2014.
David Braben's participation
In conjunction with the Buyback, and in line with the previous Buyback programme, David Braben, President and Founder, has informed the Company that he intends to sell a number of Ordinary Shares into the Buyback such that the percentage of shares, in which he and persons acting in concert with him are interested, will remain unchanged following completion of the Buyback. Mr Braben has therefore provided an irrevocable instruction to this effect to Peel Hunt. Mr Braben currently controls 32.6% of the Company's voting rights, including Ordinary Shares held by his spouse.
Further information
The Company will announce any Ordinary Share purchases made under the Buyback no later than the business day following the calendar day on which the purchase occurs. There is no certainty that the Buyback will be implemented in full or that any Ordinary Shares will be repurchased. As at the date of this announcement, the Company has 39,478,535 Ordinary Shares in issue, of which 36,960,008 carry voting rights, following the deduction of shares held in treasury.
Enquiries
:
Frontier Developments
IR@frontier.co.uk
+44 (0)1223 394300
Peel Hunt - Nomad and Corporate Broker
Neil Patel / Ben Cryer / Kate Bannatyne
+44 (0)20 7418 8900
About Frontier Developments plc
Frontier Developments plc is a leading independent developer and publisher of video games, specialising in creative management simulation (CMS) experiences. Headquartered in Cambridge, UK, Frontier develops and nurtures globally successful game franchises, including core CMS franchises such as
Planet Coaster
,
Planet Zoo
, and
Jurassic World Evolution
, using its proprietary COBRA technology. The CMS genre underpins Frontier's strategy, which is driving long-term and sustainable growth through strong player engagement, robust back-catalogue performance, and an exciting pipeline of new games.
Frontier's LEI number: 213800B9LGPWUAZ9GX18.
www.frontier.co.uk

latest.title: Option Grants / PDMR Dealings
latest.content:
Today 10:45
RNS Number : 6197S
Frontier Developments PLC
11 February 2026
11 February 2026
Frontier Developments plc
Option Grants / PDMR Dealings
On 10 February 2026, Frontier Developments plc (AIM: FDEV, 'Frontier', the 'Company', or the 'Group'), a leading developer and publisher of video games based in Cambridge, UK
,
granted a total of 185,000 options over Ordinary Shares of 0.5p each in the Company ('Ordinary Shares') under the existing Long Term Incentive Plan ('LTIP') to three Executive Directors (the 'Options').
The exercise price of the Options under the LTIP is the nominal value of the Ordinary Shares, being 0.5p each. The Options are due to vest on 10 February 2029, with 50% of the Options vesting subject to the achievement of threshold targets for revenue, profit and shareholder return over the three-year vesting period, and the remaining 50% vesting on a straight-line basis against stretch targets for the three performance measures.
The Options awarded to the Executive Directors are set out in the table below, together with the total number of Ordinary Shares held under option by each of the Executive Directors under all share option schemes.

company_30s.what_they_do: FDEV is a video‑game developer that creates and publishes simulation titles, most famously the Jurassic World Evolution series and a sequel to the popular Planet Zoo franchise.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The gaming market is worth billions and the company owns strong IPs that keep players engaged for long periods. Its titles sit in a niche that blends strategy, creativity and animal‑management, giving it a defensible position against larger competitors.
company_30s.current_state: FDEV has recently rolled out a new Jurassic World title and announced a sequel to Planet Zoo, driving fresh revenue streams. The company is still burning cash to support development, but it has begun a share‑buyback program and a new CEO has taken the helm. The share price remains very low compared to past highs.

rns_narrative: The company’s story is now dominated by a fresh wave of releases – Jurassic World Evolution 3 and a Planet Zoo sequel – which have lifted user engagement and generated positive earnings in the first half of the year.
At the same time FDEV has been clearing out board ownership, completing a £10 million buy‑back, and bringing in a new CEO to steer the next growth phase.
These moves suggest the firm is stabilising: it has new cash‑flow drivers, a fresh leadership team, and a clear plan to monetise its IPs.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish chatter focuses on the strong reception of the new games and the upgraded outlook, arguing that the share price is a bargain.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to heavy board selling, a large cash burn, and the risk that future titles might not repeat past success.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The community was once indifferent; it has now become slightly optimistic as releases roll out, yet the conversation remains muted.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – there is no current public buzz, so a catalyst could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: The release of the Jurassic World Evolution 3 sequel in the next month could lift user activity and revenue.
A Q2 earnings announcement that beats expectations would confirm the company’s improved cash flow.
Completion of the share‑buyback program could signal confidence from management and tighten the supply of shares.
Any new IP announcement or partnership that expands the franchise ecosystem would add upside.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a breakout above the 52‑week low with volume at least twice the average, a sudden spike in daily trades, or a positive earnings surprise. A sustained rally above the support zone would confirm mean reversion.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still in the early phase of a deep crash bottom. The APEX score indicates a tight squeeze, so a small catalyst could trigger a jump. Confirmation will come from volume and a break of the low‑range support.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock is down roughly 86 % from its peak – a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically delivers the biggest rebounds. If the price recovers to the midpoint between its current level and its previous high, that’s about a 300 % upside. The upside far outweighs the limited downside, which is capped at the current price.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company’s cash burn continues unchecked or if the new releases fail to generate the expected revenue, the share could fall further. A sustained decline would erode the 300 % upside and leave investors with a steep loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price; potential gain is roughly 3 × that amount, giving a risk‑reward ratio of at least 1:3.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price closes above the 52‑week low with volume at least 2× the 20‑day average. Size the position so that the stop‑loss (just below the support level) would cap losses at the current price. Monitor earnings and release dates for confirmation.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the stock tests the 52‑week low again and volume spikes, or until a new game launch is announced. A clear break above that level signals a mean‑reversion start.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks are evident; the company is not delisting or filing for bankruptcy. Therefore, a passing scenario is unlikely.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With an 86 % crash, a fresh wave of game releases, and no immediate terminal risk, FDEV presents a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play that could deliver a 300 % upside if the mean‑reversion kicks in.
